headline: US-Iran war enters third week as Hormuz blockade drives oil above $100/bbl, IEA unleashes record 400M-barrel SPR release while Israel plans largest Lebanon ground invasion since 2006

top_stories.title: US-Iran War Day 16: Kharg Island Bombed, Hormuz Blockade Holds
top_stories.summary: Operation Epic Fury entered its 16th day with US strikes on Iran's Kharg Island, the country's primary oil export terminal. Iran's air defense and navy have been largely destroyed, with drone capability down 95%. US casualties stand at 13 killed and 200 wounded. Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, who replaced his assassinated father on March 9, has hardened the blockade stance, vowing the strait will reopen only when strikes cease.
top_stories.investor_impact: Extended military engagement with no clear endpoint. War costs mounting for the US alongside $175B tariff refund liability from SCOTUS ruling. Defense stocks outperform but broader market faces prolonged risk-off. Brent at $103/bbl with $120+ scenario if blockade persists.

top_stories.title: Strait of Hormuz Physical Closure Removes 20% of Global Oil Supply
top_stories.summary: The Hormuz blockade has effectively shut down ~20% of global oil supply, with near-zero commercial traffic since February 28. The IEA responded with a record 400M-barrel strategic petroleum reserve release, the largest coordinated drawdown in history. Brent crude surged to $103.14/bbl while WTI reached $98.71. Gulf states face a paradox: record oil prices but physically blocked export routes. Kuwait and Qatar face near-total economic paralysis with no pipeline alternatives.
top_stories.investor_impact: Most severe energy supply disruption since 1973. SPR release provides 30-60 days of price relief but cannot offset full Hormuz closure indefinitely. LNG supplies from Qatar completely offline. Energy importers face current account deterioration and inflation surge. Non-Hormuz exporters (Norway, Brazil, Nigeria) are primary beneficiaries.

top_stories.title: Israel-Hezbollah War Escalates: Largest Ground Invasion of Lebanon Since 2006 Planned
top_stories.summary: Israel's conflict with Hezbollah has killed 826+ people in Lebanon (including 98 children) and displaced approximately 1 million. The IDF's 91st Division is conducting active ground operations, with Israel planning its largest invasion south of the Litani River since 2006. Critically, Israel is running low on missile interceptors, prompting an $826M emergency procurement. Lebanon has outlawed Hezbollah's military activities.
top_stories.investor_impact: Multi-front Israeli engagement strains defense budget and raises insurance costs for regional shipping. Interceptor shortage creates vulnerability to sustained rocket attacks. Regional instability premium elevated across MENA assets.

top_stories.title: Russia Launches Record 480-Drone Barrage on Ukraine While Losing Territory
top_stories.summary: Russia launched its largest drone attack of the war — 480 drones plus 68 missiles targeting Ukrainian energy infrastructure on March 6-7. Despite this escalation, Russia has lost 57 square miles of territory in four weeks. Russia confirmed supplying Iran with Shahed drones, deepening its involvement in the Middle East conflict. The EU extended individual sanctions against Russia for six months. Peace efforts remain stalled.
top_stories.investor_impact: Russia benefits from oil windfall at $103/bbl via non-Hormuz routes. Ukraine's energy infrastructure faces renewed pressure heading into spring. EU sanctions extension maintains investment restrictions. Russia-Iran drone supply link creates sanctions escalation risk.

top_stories.title: Global Trade Regime in Upheaval: SCOTUS Tariff Ruling, Section 301 Investigations, US Jobs Lost
top_stories.summary: The US trade policy landscape shifted dramatically as Section 122 global tariffs of 10% replaced the SCOTUS-struck IEEPA tariffs, while USTR launched the broadest-ever Section 301 investigations against 16 economies. US-China tariffs hit record levels (47.5%/31.9%). The US economy lost 92,000 jobs in February with unemployment at 4.4%, as Goldman Sachs warned of stagflation. Trump threatened a trade embargo against Spain. North Korea fired 10 ballistic missiles on March 14.
top_stories.investor_impact: Trade policy uncertainty at extreme levels. Section 301 investigations threaten additional tariffs on 16 economies. US-Canada steel/aluminum tariffs doubled to 50%. Stagflation risk rising with oil shock plus tariff-driven price increases. Fed faces impossible choice at March 17-18 meeting.

regional_summaries.Middle East & North Africa: Epicenter of the global crisis. Iran under sustained bombardment with new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei hardening Hormuz blockade stance. Gulf states face paradox of record oil prices but blocked export routes — Kuwait and Qatar near economic paralysis. Israel planning largest Lebanon ground invasion since 2006 while critically low on interceptors. Egypt and Jordan absorb refugee spillover and face imported inflation.
regional_summaries.North America: US faces multi-front crisis: Iran war costs escalating, SCOTUS tariff ruling creating policy chaos, 92K jobs lost in February, stagflation warnings from Goldman Sachs. Section 301 investigations launched against 16 economies. Fed meeting March 17-18 amid impossible macro conditions. Canada benefits from elevated oil prices and USMCA exemptions. Mexico navigating tariff uncertainty.
regional_summaries.Europe: Energy crisis fears intensify as Hormuz closure and Suez/Bab el-Mandeb disruption reignite 2022-era concerns. Natural gas prices surged from EUR30 to EUR48/MWh. Germany passed record $107B defense budget. France municipal elections saw National Rally post best-ever results. EU extended Russia sanctions and bypassed Hungary veto on Ukraine funding. Central bank 'super week' approaches.
regional_summaries.East Asia: LNG crisis from Hormuz closure hits hardest here. Japan and South Korea are most exposed energy importers. Japan announced record $58B defense budget (+9.4%) while THAAD was redeployed from South Korea to Middle East, creating security gap. South Korea targeting 3.5% GDP defense spending, Taiwan targeting 5% with $11.1B US arms sale. China's Two Sessions focused on AI and technological self-reliance. North Korea fired 10 ballistic missiles.
regional_summaries.South Asia: India negotiating Hormuz passage for essential imports; oil at $103/bbl adds severe pressure to import bill. Bangladesh inaugurated new PM Tarique Rahman after landslide. Pakistan faces border tensions and imported energy inflation. Sri Lanka vulnerable to Gulf supply disruption.
regional_summaries.Latin America: Chile inaugurated Jose Antonio Kast — sharpest rightward shift since Pinochet. Argentina's recession continues under Milei but Vaca Muerta energy exports increasingly valuable at $103/bbl. Brazil benefits as net oil exporter with non-Hormuz routes. Colombia faces political uncertainty under Petro. Peru presidential elections approaching April 12.
regional_summaries.Sub-Saharan Africa: Nigeria and Ghana face spiking energy costs with limited fiscal buffers. Sudan crisis deepens — world's worst humanitarian emergency with 25M facing hunger, ceasefire rejected. DRC/Rwanda conflict escalates with US sanctioning Rwanda Defence Force. Ethiopia sees violent TPLF factional clashes. South Africa hit by record marine gas oil prices.
regional_summaries.Central Asia: Kazakhstan and Azerbaijan are top beneficiaries of Hormuz crisis — CPC and BTC pipelines bypass Hormuz entirely, delivering windfall revenues. Uzbekistan benefits from Russian economic spillover. The region's pipeline-based energy exports attract growing strategic interest from importers seeking supply diversification.
regional_summaries.Southeast Asia: Chokepoint exposure elevated across the region. Singapore port congestion rising from trade rerouting. Malacca Strait congested with 750+ ships queuing from Hormuz spillover. Myanmar junta controls only 21% of territory with resistance advancing toward Mandalay. Vietnam held legislative elections with 99.6% turnout. Philippines, Thailand face imported inflation pressure.
regional_summaries.Oceania: Australia and New Zealand relatively insulated from Hormuz crisis. Australia benefits as indirect beneficiary: LNG premium, gold mining windfall at record $5,019/oz, commodities elevated. New Zealand faces modest imported inflation but limited direct exposure.

market_context.summary: Global markets in risk-off mode driven by Hormuz closure and multi-front conflict. Oil surged above $100/bbl for the first time since 2022, gold shattered records at $5,019.70/oz on safe-haven demand. Equities broadly lower with S&P 500 at 5,632 (-0.61%) and Nikkei 53,820 (-1.16%). Bond yields reflected mixed signals with US 10Y at 4.28% amid stagflation fears. Central bank decisions imminent (Fed Mar 17-18, BOJ Mar 18-19).

notable_moves.driver: Hormuz blockade — $103.14/bbl, 20% of global supply disrupted
Record $5,019.70/oz on safe-haven flows and geopolitical risk premium
Risk-off: Iran war, stagflation fears, tariff uncertainty
Energy import costs, yen weakness, THAAD redeployment concerns
European energy crisis fears, gas at EUR48/MWh
Brazil benefits as net oil exporter with non-Hormuz routes
4.28% yield — stagflation vs flight-to-safety tension; Fed decision Mar 17-18
EUR30 to EUR48/MWh on LNG supply disruption from Qatar via Hormuz

watchlist_updates.summary: War Day 16. Kharg Island bombed. New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei hardening stance. Economy in freefall. Internet shutdown. Completely uninvestable.
War costs escalating with no endpoint. SCOTUS tariff chaos. 92K jobs lost. Goldman warns of stagflation. Fed faces impossible choice March 17-18.
Multi-front war: Iran strikes plus Lebanon ground invasion planned. Critically low on missile interceptors. $826M emergency procurement.
No pipeline alternative to Hormuz. Near-total economic paralysis despite record oil prices. Chokepoint exposure at maximum.
World's largest LNG exporter completely offline. Hormuz blockade blocks all maritime trade. Revenue disruption severe.
Record 480-drone barrage on energy infrastructure. Despite territorial gains, infrastructure vulnerability increasing.
Non-Hormuz oil exporter with record revenues at $103/bbl. Primary energy crisis beneficiary in Europe.
Net oil exporter, geographically insulated from Hormuz. Bovespa +0.83% — only major index in green.
CPC pipeline bypasses Hormuz entirely. Oil windfall revenues. Growing strategic interest from energy importers.
New President Jose Antonio Kast inaugurated March 11. Sharpest rightward shift since Pinochet. Market-friendly but polarizing.
New PM Tarique Rahman (BNP) sworn in after landslide. Political transition from Yunus interim government. Policy direction uncertain.

data_quality_summary.notes: Full pipeline run with 2,946 validated updates (2,685 direct + 261 signals). Pre-fetched data from World Bank, IMF, GDELT, FX/commodities. 90 news events collected. 605 trend estimates across 55 countries. 16 escalations resolved (all accepted). 137 total alerts (21 critical). Oil crisis dominates all regions.
